opp.name: Symrise [S2C, Intake] April'26
opp.next_step: Hi Andre, I hope you're doing well and that your travels have been going well. I wanted to follow up on the information I shared after our discussion regarding how AI can support procurement at Symrise, particularly around iRisk and our contract analysis/contracting agents. I know we discussed taking some time to review the materials internally before determining next steps, so I wanted to see if you had any feedback or questions that I could help address. If there is continued interest, I'd be happy to coordinate a brief scoping session with one of our solution consultants to tailor a demonstration focused on your priorities and discuss how these capabilities could fit into Symrise's procurement roadmap. Please let me know what works best for your team. I look forward to hearing your thoughts and continuing the conversation.
opp.ais_why:
• Rep is sending across the presentation plus case studies covering ANA, Contract Management, and Risk Management.
• Description is empty and there is no transcript or Avoma call. The ANA reference is rep-led, not customer-voice excitement.
• Qualified, $110K. Customer Andre is reviewing internally before scheduling a demo. Low engagement intensity.

sources: Products__c field (iContract, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA)
NextStep field (case studies for ANA, Contract, Risk)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI target has been documented. The product scope (S2C: iContract, iRisk; Intake: Merlin Intake, ANA) signals procurement contract/risk management and intake automation pain, but no buyer has articulated savings, cycle-time reduction, error-rate improvement, or headcount efficiency on any call or in any Salesforce field. The next step references case studies for ANA, Contract Management, and Risk Management, implying the buyer wants to understand value, but no metrics have been captured yet. The move is to use the upcoming demo to quantify the buyer's current-state pain and co-build a savings/efficiency model tied to their volume (contract count, requisition throughput, risk exposure).

sources: NextStep field (refers to buyer contact, title/role unknown)
Account contacts (senior procurement leaders, not confirmed on this opp)
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. Zero buyer-side OpportunityContactRoles exist, so no individual is formally tied to this opportunity. The NextStep field refers to 'Andre' (buyer contact) who will 'review and get back post his internal discussion to setup a demo,' indicating an active point of contact, but no title, role, or evidence of advocacy is recorded. It is unclear whether this contact is an influencer, a coach, or a gatekeeper. No Avoma calls exist to reveal champion behavior (introductions, pushing our agenda, sharing internal Intel, driving urgency). The account contact list includes senior procurement leaders (Marianne Aguiar, VP; Nashrika Parasnath, Associate Category Director; Al Kraus, Procurement Director) who could sponsor the deal, but none are confirmed as engaged. The move is to assess the current contact's influence and access during the demo, then ask for an introduction to the procurement VP (Aguiar) or the Global Sourcing Sr Manager to build a true champion with EB/DM access.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been identified. The Competitors__c field is empty (unknown per ground truth), no other Salesforce field names a rival or incumbent, and no Avoma calls exist to reveal the competitive landscape. The deal is early-stage (Qualified, 78 days in), so the competitive picture may not yet be visible, or Symrise may be running a sole-source evaluation. The account contact list and product scope (S2C + Intake + AI) suggest Symrise likely has an incumbent contract management system (potentially Ariba, Coupa, or a legacy tool) and possibly a manual or basic intake process. The move is to discover the competitive set during the demo: ask what they use today for contract management, risk tracking, and intake (incumbent = competitor), whether they are evaluating other vendors in parallel, and what drove them to Zycus now. Position Merlin/ANA as the AI differentiation if competitors surface.

sources: Products__c field (iContract, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA — defines pain domains)
NextStep field (case studies for ANA, Contract, Risk — confirms interest in solving these)
identify_pain.narrative: The product scope (iContract, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA) implies three pain areas: (1) contract lifecycle management inefficiency or risk (iContract + iRisk), (2) intake/requisition bottleneck or manual routing (Merlin Intake), and (3) lack of AI-driven analytics or automation on requisitions (ANA). No buyer has articulated the pain in their own words. No Salesforce free-text field (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c) is populated, and no Avoma calls exist. The NextStep reference to 'case studies of implementation about ANA, Contract Management and Risk Management' suggests the buyer wants to see how others solved these problems, confirming the pain domains but not the pain owner or severity. Inferred pain owner: procurement operations (contract/intake volume) and risk/compliance (contract risk exposure). The move is to use the demo to get the buyer to describe their current-state pain in measurable terms (contract cycle time, requisition backlog, risk incidents, manual effort) and name who owns each pain.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting and procurement mechanics are unknown. No Salesforce field documents whether Symrise requires an MSA, SOW, or uses a procurement portal, nor whether InfoSec or legal review is standard. No partner or SI involvement is mentioned. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function exists. The buyer is a large global F&I/CPG ingredients manufacturer (Symrise), so a formal procurement process, legal redlines, and InfoSec review are highly likely, but none are confirmed. The move is to ask during the demo about their standard procurement path (RFP/RFI → legal review → InfoSec questionnaire → PO issuance) and timeline for each gate, and to flag InfoSec/legal early so we can prep the security packet and redline playbook.

sources: Account contacts (Marianne Aguiar, Thomas Kauz at VP level, not confirmed on this opp)
NextStep field (buyer to review internally, authority unclear)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. No Contact Role of Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor exists, no Salesforce free-text field names a budget authority, and no Avoma calls were discovered (zero buyer calls on record). The account contact list includes VP-level procurement (Marianne Aguiar, VP Global Procurement Excellence & Strategic Materials) and a finance VP (Thomas Kauz, VP Strategy & Finance), either of whom could hold or influence the budget for a $110k S2C+Intake deal, but neither is confirmed as engaged in this opportunity. The next step mentions internal review by the buyer contact, but that contact's title and authority are not recorded. The move is to map the approval chain during the demo: ask who controls the $110k budget, whether CFO/procurement leadership must approve, and secure an introduction to that sponsor.

sources: NextStep field (internal review, implies multi-person process)
Account contacts (procurement + finance + technical, signals cross-functional committee)
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. No Salesforce field describes the approval chain, committee structure, or timeline milestones. The NextStep field states the buyer will 'review internally and get back to setup a demo,' implying a multi-person evaluation, but the process beyond that is undocumented. No Avoma calls exist to reveal the process organically. The account contact list shows procurement (Aguiar, Parasnath, Kraus, Melo), finance (Kauz, Serbource), and technical procurement (Morozov, Newhart, Dalfonzo), suggesting a cross-functional buying committee is likely, but roles and sequence are unconfirmed. The move is to map the process during the demo: ask how they will decide (demos → RFP → shortlist → business case → CFO approval?), who participates at each gate, and what the internal timeline looks like against the Apr 2027 close date.

sources: Products__c field (iContract, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA — defines scope)
NextStep field (case studies across ANA, Contract, Risk — implies evaluation domains)
decision_criteria.narrative: The product scope (iContract for contract lifecycle, iRisk for risk management, Merlin Intake + ANA for intake automation and AI-driven requisition handling) defines the functional scope, but the buyer's evaluation criteria have not been documented. No RFP, no scored requirements matrix, no stated priorities (e.g. ease of use vs configurability, AI accuracy vs speed, ERP integration depth) appear in any field or call. The next step (case studies for ANA, Contract, Risk) signals the buyer wants proof points across all three pillars, suggesting those are the decision domains, but no weighted criteria or test-case plan exists. Inferred criteria: contract automation capability, risk detection/mitigation features, intake/requisition AI (Merlin/ANA), and likely ERP integration (Symrise operates globally, likely SAP/Oracle). The move is to capture the buyer's decision scorecard during the demo and map it to our strengths.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS (AI Score/Status/Why) data exists for this opportunity. Product scope includes Merlin Intake and ANA, and the buyer requested ANA case studies, signaling curiosity about AI-driven intake automation. No call evidence of AI appetite or resistance. Tier cannot be definitively assigned; inferred as AI Curious based on product selection and case-study request, but unconfirmed.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created and qualified 9 Apr 2026. NextStep field (current) states Ed will send presentation + case studies (ANA, Contract, Risk), buyer to review internally and schedule demo. LastActivityDate 28 May 2026 (29 days ago) indicates recent engagement. No field history of stage movement, amount change, or close-date slip. Healthy early-stage progression toward demo milestone.

north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified deal at 29 days of silence after buyer committed to internal review and demo scheduling. No documented follow-up, demo not yet scheduled. Early-stage stall risk; needs proactive re-engagement to confirm interest and advance to demo.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal with clear product scope and active next step, but significant MEDDPICC gaps and zero buyer-call history limit visibility. Immediate priorities: deliver the promised case studies, secure the demo, and use it to map EB/DM/champion, quantify pain, and discover the competitive landscape. Multi-thread to senior procurement leadership (Aguiar, Parasnath) to build executive sponsorship.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified. Competitors__c is empty, no free-text field names a rival, and zero Avoma calls exist. Early-stage (Qualified, 78 days in), so competitive landscape may not yet be visible. Symrise likely has an incumbent contract/intake system (Ariba, Coupa, or legacy) that will become the primary competitor once discovered. Move: map incumbent and any parallel evaluations during the demo.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Product scope includes Merlin Intake and ANA (AI-driven intake automation and analytics), confirming AI is in the deal. NextStep references case studies for ANA, signaling buyer interest in understanding the AI capability. No AIS score or field confirms buyer AI appetite or our positioning effectiveness. No calls exist to gauge whether the AI story is landing. Under-positioned risk: the buyer may see Merlin/ANA as optional add-ons rather than the core differentiation. The move is to anchor the AI narrative to quantified pain (requisition cycle time, spend visibility, contract risk detection) in the demo and case studies.

hard.next_step:
Hi Andre,

I hope you're doing well and that your travels have been going well.

I wanted to follow up on the information I shared after our discussion regarding how AI can support procurement at Symrise, particularly around iRisk and our contract analysis/contracting agents.

I know we discussed taking some time to review the materials internally before determining next steps, so I wanted to see if you had any feedback or questions that I could help address.

If there is continued interest, I'd be happy to coordinate a brief scoping session with one of our solution consultants to tailor a demonstration focused on your priorities and discuss how these capabilities could fit into Symrise's procurement roadmap.

Please let me know what works best for your team. I look forward to hearing your thoughts and continuing the conversation.
